name: My workers' comp denied my surgery—what is the very first thing I should do?
description: First, obtain the written denial letter to identify the specific reason for rejection. Then, contact a workers' compensation attorney to file a formal appeal or

mainEntity.name: My workers' comp denied my surgery—what is the very first thing I should do?

acceptedAnswer.text: First, obtain the written denial letter to identify the specific reason for rejection. Then, contact a workers' compensation attorney to file a formal appeal or Application for Hearing with the Labor Commission.
acceptedAnswer.description:
A denial is not the end of the road; it is the beginning of the legal dispute. In Utah and Idaho, insurance companies frequently deny expensive treatments like MRIs or surgeries to save costs. You must file a formal 'Application for Hearing' with the state labor commission to challenge the denial.

Key factors include the 'medical necessity' of the treatment and whether the insurance company’s doctor (who hasn't met you) claims the treatment isn't related to the work accident.

Check your denial letter for the 'Reason for Denial.' Most have a strict 30-day window for certain types of internal appeals, though the legal statute of limitations is longer.

If your surgery is denied, the insurance company is betting you won't fight back. A lawyer's job is to present the medical evidence that forces them to pay.
